title: Leptonychia batangensis
intro: Leptonychia batangensis is a plant species native to Indonesia, classified within the family Malvaceae and commonly known as Batangensis Mallow. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as potential wound healing and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Leptonychia batangensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its bioactivity. In traditional herbal medicine, Leptonychia batangensis is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
